1	# AGENTS.md
2	
3	## Task Completion Requirements
4	
5	- Both `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` must pass before considering tasks completed.
6	- NEVER run `bun test`. Always use `bun run test` (runs Vitest).
7	
8	## Project Snapshot
9	
10	T3 Code is a minimal web GUI for using code agents like Codex and Claude Code (coming soon).
11	
12	This repository is a VERY EARLY WIP. Proposing sweeping changes that improve long-term maintainability is encouraged.
13	
14	## Core Priorities
15	
16	1. Performance first.
17	2. Reliability first.
18	3. Keep behavior predictable under load and during failures (session restarts, reconnects, partial streams).
19	
20	If a tradeoff is required, choose correctness and robustness over short-term convenience.
21	
22	## Maintainability
23	
24	Long term maintainability is a core priority. If you add new functionality, first check if there are shared logic that can be extracted to a separate module. Duplicate logic across mulitple files is a code smell and should be avoided. Don't be afraid to change existing code. Don't take shortcuts by just adding local logic to solve a problem.
25	
26	## Package Roles
27	
28	- `apps/server`: Node.js WebSocket server. Wraps Codex app-server (JSON-RPC over stdio), serves the React web app, and manages provider sessions.
29	- `apps/web`: React/Vite UI. Owns session UX, conversation/event rendering, and client-side state. Connects to the server via WebSocket.
30	- `packages/contracts`: Shared effect/Schema schemas and TypeScript contracts for provider events, WebSocket protocol, and model/session types. Keep this package schema-only — no runtime logic.
31	- `packages/shared`: Shared runtime utilities consumed by both server and web. Uses explicit subpath exports (e.g. `@t3tools/shared/git`) — no barrel index.
32	
33	## Codex App Server (Important)
34	
35	T3 Code is currently Codex-first. The server starts `codex app-server` (JSON-RPC over stdio) per provider session, then streams structured events to the browser through WebSocket push messages.
36	
37	How we use it in this codebase:
38	
39	- Session startup/resume and turn lifecycle are brokered in `apps/server/src/codexAppServerManager.ts`.
40	- Provider dispatch and thread event logging are coordinated in `apps/server/src/providerManager.ts`.
41	- WebSocket server routes NativeApi methods in `apps/server/src/wsServer.ts`.
42	- Web app consumes orchestration domain events via WebSocket push on channel `orchestration.domainEvent` (provider runtime activity is projected into orchestration events server-side).
43	
44	Docs:
45	
46	- Codex App Server docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/sdk/#app-server
47	
48	## Reference Repos
49	
50	- Open-source Codex repo: https://github.com/openai/codex
51	- Codex-Monitor (Tauri, feature-complete, strong reference implementation): https://github.com/Dimillian/CodexMonitor
52	
53	Use these as implementation references when designing protocol handling, UX flows, and operational safeguards.
54

- _chat.index.tsx - Chat home/index page
- _chat.settings.tsx - Settings page (570+ lines with full settings UI)

Settings route (_chat.settings.tsx) includes:

- Codex App Server configuration (binary path, home path)

- Service tier configuration (auto/fast/flex)
- Response streaming toggle
- Keybindings configuration
- Safety settings (thread deletion confirmation)

- Base UI primitives with custom styling

- TypeScript with strict typing

- @tanstack/react-router - Routing

- @base-ui/react - UI primitives

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: apps/web Application

### 1. **Dependencies & Scripts**

**Key dependencies:**
- **React ecosystem**: React 19, React DOM 19
- **Router**: TanStack React Router v1.160.2
- **State Management**: Zustand v5.0.11 (for main app state)
- **Data Fetching**: TanStack React Query v5.90.0
- **Component Library**: @base-ui/react v1.2.0 (headless components)
- **UI Icons**: lucide-react v0.564.0
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4.0.0, class-variance-authority (CVA) v0.7.1, tailwind-merge v3.4.0
- **Editors**: Lexical rich text editor (v0.41.0)
- **Terminal**: xterm (v6.0.0)
- **Diffs**: @pierre/diffs (diffing visualization)
- **Utilities**: Effect (effect v0.x - for functional programming)

**Build & Dev:**
- **Vite** v8.0.0-beta.12 (with Tailwind CSS Vite plugin)
- **TypeScript** (via catalog)
- **Vitest** for testing
- **Playwright** for browser testing
- **React Compiler** for optimization

**Scripts:**
- `dev` - Vite dev server
- `build` - Vite production build
- `preview` - Preview built assets
- `typecheck` - TypeScript checking
- `test` - Vitest run
- `test:browser` - Browser-based tests with Playwright

---

### 2. **Directory Structure**

```
apps/web/src/
├── components/
│   ├── ui/              # UI component library (60 files)
│   │   ├── button.tsx
│   │   ├── card.tsx
│   │   ├── sidebar.tsx

│   │   ├── sheet.tsx

│   │   ├── scroll-area.tsx
│   │   └── ... (30+ more UI components)
│   └── [non-ui components]
│       ├── BranchToolbar.tsx
│       ├── ChatView.tsx
│       ├── GitActionsControl.tsx
│       ├── ComposerPromptEditor.tsx
│       ├── PlanSidebar.tsx
│       ├── ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx
│       └── ... (20+ more feature components)
├── hooks/               # Custom React hooks
│   ├── useTheme.ts      # Theme management
│   ├── useMediaQuery.ts
│   └── useTurnDiffSummaries.ts
├── lib/                 # Utilities & helpers
│   ├── utils.ts         # cn() utility for Tailwind/CVA merging
│   ├── serverReactQuery.ts
│   ├── projectReactQuery.ts
│   ├── providerReactQuery.ts
│   ├── gitReactQuery.ts
│   └── ... (13 files total)
├── routes/              # TanStack Router routes
│   ├── __root.tsx       # Root layout/provider setup
│   ├── _chat.tsx
│   ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx
│   ├── _chat.index.tsx
│   └── _chat.settings.tsx
├── main.tsx             # Entry point
├── index.css            # Global styles with Tailwind & CSS variables
├── router.ts            # Router configuration
├── store.ts             # Zustand store (Zustand + localStorage)
├── types.ts             # Type definitions
├── appSettings.ts       # App settings with localStorage persistence
├── composerDraftStore.ts # Composer draft state management
├── terminalStateStore.ts # Terminal state management
└── ... (79 files total - mix of logic, utilities, tests)
```

---

### 3. **App Structure & Entry Point**

**Entry Point: `/apps/web/src/main.tsx`**
- Creates React root with `ReactDOM.createRoot()`
- Configures history (hash-based for Electron, browser-based for web)
- Wraps app with `RouterProvider` from TanStack React Router

**Root Route: `/apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`**
- Sets up provider hierarchy:
  ```
  RouterProvider
    └── QueryClientProvider (React Query)
        └── StoreProvider (Zustand + effect cleanup)
            └── ToastProvider
                └── AnchoredToastProvider
                    └── EventRouter (handles server events)
                        └── DesktopProjectBootstrap
                            └── Outlet (nested routes)
  ```
- Handles server synchronization via WebSocket
- Manages error states and displays error boundary UI
- Loads initial data from orchestration service

---

### 4. **State Management & Context Providers**

**1. Zustand Store** (`/apps/web/src/store.ts`) - Main app state
   - **State keys**: `projects`, `threads`, `threadsHydrated`
   - **Persistence**: localStorage with key `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - **Actions**: 
     - `syncServerReadModel` - Hydrate from server
     - `markThreadVisited/Unread` - Thread visit tracking
     - `toggleProject` / `setProjectExpanded` - Project expansion state
     - `setError` - Thread error state
     - `setThreadBranch` - Git branch/worktree management
   - **Pattern**: Pure state transition functions + Zustand store hook

**2. App Settings Store** (`/apps/web/src/appSettings.ts`)
   - **Storage key**: `t3code:app-settings:v1`
   - **State**: 
     - `codexBinaryPath`, `codexHomePath`
     - `confirmThreadDelete`, `enableAssistantStreaming`
     - `codexServiceTier` (auto/fast/flex)
     - `customCodexModels` (array)
   - **Pattern**: `useSyncExternalStore` hook + localStorage listener
   - **Schema validation**: Using Effect Schema

**3. Composer Draft Store** (`/apps/web/src/composerDraftStore.ts`)
   - **Storage key**: `t3code:composer-drafts:v1`
   - **State**: 
     - Draft threads (per thread ID)
     - Composer prompts & image attachments
     - Draft thread environment mode (local/worktree)
   - **Pattern**: Zustand with `persist` middleware for localStorage

**4. Terminal State Store** (`/apps/web/src/terminalStateStore.ts`)
   - In-memory Zustand store (not persisted)
   - Tracks terminal activity per thread

**5. Theme Store** (`/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`)
   - **Storage key**: `t3code:theme`
   - **Options**: "light" | "dark" | "system"
   - **Pattern**: `useSyncExternalStore` + localStorage + matchMedia listener
   - **DOM manipulation**: Adds/removes `.dark` class on `<html>` element
   - **Smooth transitions**: Suppresses animations during theme switch with `.no-transitions` class

---

### 5. **Styling Approach**

**Methodology: Tailwind CSS 4.0 + CSS Custom Properties + Headless Components**

**CSS Structure** (`/apps/web/src/index.css`):

1. **Tailwind imports** at the top (`@import "tailwindcss"`)

2. **Custom variants**:
   ```css
   @custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *));
   ```
   - Dark mode applied via `.dark` class on HTML element

3. **Theme variables defined in `:root`** and `@variant dark`:
   ```css
   --background, --foreground
   --primary, --primary-foreground
   --secondary, --secondary-foreground
   --destructive, --destructive-foreground
   --accent, --accent-foreground
   --info, --info-foreground
   --success, --success-foreground
   --warning, --warning-foreground
   --border, --input, --ring
   --muted, --muted-foreground
   --card, --card-foreground
   --popover, --popover-foreground
   --radius (and radius-sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl)
   ```

4. **Color system**: 
   - Uses `oklch()` color space for primary colors
   - Includes semantic color names
   - Light mode: white backgrounds, neutral-800 text
   - Dark mode: neutral-950 background with color-mix adjustments

5. **Utility classes**:
   - Using Tailwind's @apply with theme variables
   - Custom animations (skeleton animation)
   - Radius variants calculated from base radius

6. **Component styling**:
   - Scoped `.chat-markdown` styles for rendered content
   - Terminal-specific scrollbar styling
   - Drag region styling for Electron titlebar
   - Diff panel theme bridging

**Component Pattern** (e.g., `button.tsx`, `card.tsx`):
- Use `cva` (class-variance-authority) for component variants
- Use `cn()` utility function (merges Tailwind + CVA classes)
- Use `@base-ui/react` utilities:
  - `useRender` - polymorphic component render prop
  - `mergeProps` - props merging for accessibility
- Extensive use of data attributes (`data-slot`) for styling hooks

**Example Button Component**:
```tsx
const buttonVariants = cva("inline-flex shrink-0...", {
  variants: {
    size: { default: "h-9 px-3", icon: "size-9", ... },
    variant: { 
      default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground",
      outline: "border-input bg-popover",
      ghost: "border-transparent"
    }
  }
});

function Button({ className, variant, size, ...props }) {
  return useRender({
    defaultTagName: "button",
    props: mergeProps(
      { className: cn(buttonVariants({ className, size, variant })) },
      props
    ),
    render
  });
}
```

---

### 6. **Existing Theme-Related Code**

**Theme Hook** (`/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`):
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`
- `theme` - raw setting ("light" | "dark" | "system")
- `resolvedTheme` - computed ("light" | "dark")
- `setTheme()` - updates localStorage and DOM

**Theme Application**:
- Adds/removes `.dark` class on `<html>` element
- Listens to system preference changes via `matchMedia`
- Listens to other tabs' storage changes
- Suppresses transitions during theme switch with `.no-transitions` class

**CSS Variables for Dark Mode**:
- All theme colors are CSS variables
- Dark mode variant in index.css adjusts variables with color-mix for harmony
- Components reference variables, not hardcoded colors

**No existing dark mode toggle UI component** - just the hook and CSS infrastructure

---

### 7. **Component Library & UI Components**

**Base UI Library**: @base-ui/react (headless, accessibility-focused)

**60+ UI components in `/components/ui/`**:
- Layout: `sidebar`, `card`, `sheet`, `dialog`, `popover`
- Forms: `input`, `button`, `label`, `checkbox`, `switch`, `radio-group`, `toggle`, `field`
- Feedback: `toast`, `alert`, `tooltip`, `spinner`, `skeleton`
- Navigation: `menu`, `command`, `combobox`
- Content: `scroll-area`, `separator`, `badge`, `group`, `kbd`
- Advanced: `alert-dialog`, `input-group`, `toggle-group`

**Styling Pattern**:
- All use CVA for variants
- All use `cn()` for class merging
- Most use `@base-ui/react` utilities (`useRender`, `mergeProps`)
- CSS variables for colors/spacing
- Tailwind utilities for layout/positioning

---

### 8. **localStorage Usage Patterns**

**All apps using localStorage for state persistence:**

1. **`store.ts`** - App state:
   - Key: `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - Data: `{ expandedProjectCwds: string[] }`
   - Legacy key cleanup for old versions

2. **`appSettings.ts`** - User settings:
   - Key: `t3code:app-settings:v1`
   - Data: JSON serialized app settings with Effect Schema validation

3. **`composerDraftStore.ts`** - Composer drafts:
   - Key: `t3code:composer-drafts:v1`
   - Data: Draft threads, prompts, attachments
   - Uses Zustand's `persist` middleware

4. **`useTheme.ts`** - Theme preference:
   - Key: `t3code:theme`
   - Data: "light" | "dark" | "system"

5. **`sidebar.tsx`** - Sidebar state:
   - Cookie-based OR localStorage (configurable)
   - Tracks expand/collapse state
   - Resizable width state

**Pattern**:
- Try/catch error handling for quota issues
- Caching mechanisms to avoid excessive reads
- Storage event listeners for multi-tab sync
- Versioned keys for migrations
- Graceful fallbacks if localStorage unavailable

---

### 9. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `main.tsx` | Entry point, router setup |
| `router.ts` | Router config with query client |
| `index.css` | Global styles, Tailwind, CSS variables |
| `store.ts` | Main Zustand store + localStorage persistence |
| `appSettings.ts` | App settings with Schema validation |
| `composerDraftStore.ts` | Draft management with Zustand persist |
| `hooks/useTheme.ts` | Theme management hook |
| `routes/__root.tsx` | Root layout, provider setup, error handling |
| `components/ui/*.tsx` | 60 UI components (headless + Tailwind) |
| `lib/utils.ts` | `cn()` utility for class merging |

---

### 10. **Architecture Highlights**

- **Zero-CSS-in-JS**: All styling via Tailwind + CSS variables, no styled-components/emotion
- **Headless Components**: @base-ui/react for logic, full styling control
- **External Store Pattern**: useSyncExternalStore for theme/settings hooks
- **Zustand for Client State**: App state, drafts, terminal state
- **React Query**: Server state management
- **TanStack Router**: File-based routing with context
- **No Provider Hell**: Minimal context hierarchy in __root.tsx
- **Monorepo-aware**: Workspace imports from @t3tools packages

---

This is a modern, well-structured React application following current best practices (React 19, Tailwind 4.0, headless UI, functional patterns with Effect, etc.).

  ```
                    └── EventRouter (handles server events)
                        └── DesktopProjectBootstrap
                            └── Outlet (nested routes)
  ```
- Handles server synchronization via WebSocket
- Manages error states and displays error boundary UI
- Loads initial data from orchestration service

---

### 4. **State Management & Context Providers**

**1. Zustand Store** (`/apps/web/src/store.ts`) - Main app state
   - **State keys**: `projects`, `threads`, `threadsHydrated`
   - **Persistence**: localStorage with key `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - **Actions**: 
     - `syncServerReadModel` - Hydrate from server
     - `markThreadVisited/Unread` - Thread visit tracking
     - `toggleProject` / `setProjectExpanded` - Project expansion state
     - `setError` - Thread error state
     - `setThreadBranch` - Git branch/worktree management
   - **Pattern**: Pure state transition functions + Zustand store hook

**2. App Settings Store** (`/apps/web/src/appSettings.ts`)
   - **Storage key**: `t3code:app-settings:v1`
   - **State**: 
     - `codexBinaryPath`, `codexHomePath`
     - `confirmThreadDelete`, `enableAssistantStreaming`
     - `codexServiceTier` (auto/fast/flex)
     - `customCodexModels` (array)
   - **Pattern**: `useSyncExternalStore` hook + localStorage listener
   - **Schema validation**: Using Effect Schema

**3. Composer Draft Store** (`/apps/web/src/composerDraftStore.ts`)
   - **Storage key**: `t3code:composer-drafts:v1`
   - **State**: 
     - Draft threads (per thread ID)
     - Composer prompts & image attachments
     - Draft thread environment mode (local/worktree)
   - **Pattern**: Zustand with `persist` middleware for localStorage

**4. Terminal State Store** (`/apps/web/src/terminalStateStore.ts`)
   - In-memory Zustand store (not persisted)
   - Tracks terminal activity per thread

**5. Theme Store** (`/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`)
   - **Storage key**: `t3code:theme`
   - **Options**: "light" | "dark" | "system"
   - **Pattern**: `useSyncExternalStore` + localStorage + matchMedia listener
   - **DOM manipulation**: Adds/removes `.dark` class on `<html>` element
   - **Smooth transitions**: Suppresses animations during theme switch with `.no-transitions` class

---

### 5. **Styling Approach**

**Methodology: Tailwind CSS 4.0 + CSS Custom Properties + Headless Components**

**CSS Structure** (`/apps/web/src/index.css`):

1. **Tailwind imports** at the top (`@import "tailwindcss"`)

2. **Custom variants**:
   ```css
   @custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *));
   ```
   - Dark mode applied via `.dark` class on HTML element

3. **Theme variables defined in `:root`** and `@variant dark`:
   ```css
   --background, --foreground
   --primary, --primary-foreground
   --secondary, --secondary-foreground
   --destructive, --destructive-foreground
   --accent, --accent-foreground
   --info, --info-foreground
   --success, --success-foreground
   --warning, --warning-foreground
   --border, --input, --ring
   --muted, --muted-foreground
   --card, --card-foreground
   --popover, --popover-foreground
   --radius (and radius-sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl)
   ```

4. **Color system**: 
   - Uses `oklch()` color space for primary colors
   - Includes semantic color names
   - Light mode: white backgrounds, neutral-800 text
   - Dark mode: neutral-950 background with color-mix adjustments

5. **Utility classes**:
   - Using Tailwind's @apply with theme variables
   - Custom animations (skeleton animation)
   - Radius variants calculated from base radius

6. **Component styling**:
   - Scoped `.chat-markdown` styles for rendered content
   - Terminal-specific scrollbar styling
   - Drag region styling for Electron titlebar
   - Diff panel theme bridging

**Component Pattern** (e.g., `button.tsx`, `card.tsx`):
- Use `cva` (class-variance-authority) for component variants
- Use `cn()` utility function (merges Tailwind + CVA classes)
- Use `@base-ui/react` utilities:
  - `useRender` - polymorphic component render prop
  - `mergeProps` - props merging for accessibility
- Extensive use of data attributes (`data-slot`) for styling hooks

**Example Button Component**:
```tsx
const buttonVariants = cva("inline-flex shrink-0...", {
  variants: {
    size: { default: "h-9 px-3", icon: "size-9", ... },
    variant: { 
      default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground",
      outline: "border-input bg-popover",
      ghost: "border-transparent"
    }
  }
});

function Button({ className, variant, size, ...props }) {
  return useRender({
    defaultTagName: "button",
    props: mergeProps(
      { className: cn(buttonVariants({ className, size, variant })) },
      props
    ),
    render
  });
}
```

---

### 6. **Existing Theme-Related Code**

**Theme Hook** (`/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`):
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`
- `theme` - raw setting ("light" | "dark" | "system")
- `resolvedTheme` - computed ("light" | "dark")
- `setTheme()` - updates localStorage and DOM

**Theme Application**:
- Adds/removes `.dark` class on `<html>` element
- Listens to system preference changes via `matchMedia`
- Listens to other tabs' storage changes
- Suppresses transitions during theme switch with `.no-transitions` class

**CSS Variables for Dark Mode**:
- All theme colors are CSS variables
- Dark mode variant in index.css adjusts variables with color-mix for harmony
- Components reference variables, not hardcoded colors

**No existing dark mode toggle UI component** - just the hook and CSS infrastructure

---

### 7. **Component Library & UI Components**

**Base UI Library**: @base-ui/react (headless, accessibility-focused)

**60+ UI components in `/components/ui/`**:
- Layout: `sidebar`, `card`, `sheet`, `dialog`, `popover`
- Forms: `input`, `button`, `label`, `checkbox`, `switch`, `radio-group`, `toggle`, `field`
- Feedback: `toast`, `alert`, `tooltip`, `spinner`, `skeleton`
- Navigation: `menu`, `command`, `combobox`
- Content: `scroll-area`, `separator`, `badge`, `group`, `kbd`
- Advanced: `alert-dialog`, `input-group`, `toggle-group`

**Styling Pattern**:
- All use CVA for variants
- All use `cn()` for class merging
- Most use `@base-ui/react` utilities (`useRender`, `mergeProps`)
- CSS variables for colors/spacing
- Tailwind utilities for layout/positioning

---

### 8. **localStorage Usage Patterns**

**All apps using localStorage for state persistence:**

1. **`store.ts`** - App state:
   - Key: `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - Data: `{ expandedProjectCwds: string[] }`
   - Legacy key cleanup for old versions

2. **`appSettings.ts`** - User settings:
   - Key: `t3code:app-settings:v1`
   - Data: JSON serialized app settings with Effect Schema validation

3. **`composerDraftStore.ts`** - Composer drafts:
   - Key: `t3code:composer-drafts:v1`
   - Data: Draft threads, prompts, attachments
   - Uses Zustand's `persist` middleware

4. **`useTheme.ts`** - Theme preference:
   - Key: `t3code:theme`
   - Data: "light" | "dark" | "system"

5. **`sidebar.tsx`** - Sidebar state:
   - Cookie-based OR localStorage (configurable)
   - Tracks expand/collapse state
   - Resizable width state

**Pattern**:
- Try/catch error handling for quota issues
- Caching mechanisms to avoid excessive reads
- Storage event listeners for multi-tab sync
- Versioned keys for migrations
- Graceful fallbacks if localStorage unavailable

---

### 9. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `main.tsx` | Entry point, router setup |
| `router.ts` | Router config with query client |
| `index.css` | Global styles, Tailwind, CSS variables |
| `store.ts` | Main Zustand store + localStorage persistence |
| `appSettings.ts` | App settings with Schema validation |
| `composerDraftStore.ts` | Draft management with Zustand persist |
| `hooks/useTheme.ts` | Theme management hook |
| `routes/__root.tsx` | Root layout, provider setup, error handling |
| `components/ui/*.tsx` | 60 UI components (headless + Tailwind) |
| `lib/utils.ts` | `cn()` utility for class merging |

---

### 10. **Architecture Highlights**

- **Zero-CSS-in-JS**: All styling via Tailwind + CSS variables, no styled-components/emotion
- **Headless Components**: @base-ui/react for logic, full styling control
- **External Store Pattern**: useSyncExternalStore for theme/settings hooks
- **Zustand for Client State**: App state, drafts, terminal state
- **React Query**: Server state management
- **TanStack Router**: File-based routing with context
- **No Provider Hell**: Minimal context hierarchy in __root.tsx
- **Monorepo-aware**: Workspace imports from @t3tools packages

---

This is a modern, well-structured React application following current best practices (React 19, Tailwind 4.0, headless UI, functional patterns with Effect, etc.).

- **CSS Framework:** Tailwind CSS 4.0 (via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin)
- **Styling Pattern:** Component classes + utility-first design
- **Dark Mode:** CSS custom variant using `.dark` class selector

### 4.2 CSS Variables Theme System
**Root Theme Variables (index.css):**
```css
:root {
  color-scheme: light;
  --radius: 0.625rem;
  --background: var(--color-white);
  --foreground: var(--color-neutral-800);
  --primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264);
  --secondary: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%);
  /* ... 20+ color/radius variables */
}

@variant dark {
  color-scheme: dark;
  --background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-neutral-950) 95%, var(--color-white));
  /* ... overrides */
}
```

**Custom Tailwind Theme Integration:**
- Defined via `@theme inline` block in CSS
- Maps Tailwind colors (`--color-*`) to CSS variables
- Radius variants: `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, etc.

## 5. Key Build Configuration

### 5.1 Root package.json Scripts
```json
{
  "dev": "node scripts/dev-runner.ts dev",
  "dev:server": "node scripts/dev-runner.ts dev:server",
  "dev:web": "node scripts/dev-runner.ts dev:web",
  "dev:desktop": "node scripts/dev-runner.ts dev:desktop",
  "build": "turbo run build",
  "typecheck": "turbo run typecheck",
  "test": "turbo run test"
}
```

### 5.2 Vite Configuration (apps/web)
```typescript
plugins: [
  tanstackRouter(),
  react({ babel: { plugins: [["babel-plugin-react-compiler", { target: "19" }]] } }),
  tailwindcss(),
]
```
- Uses **TanStack Router** for routing

- **Tailwind CSS 4.0** via Vite plugin

### 5.3 Turbo Task Dependencies
- `dev` depends on `@t3tools/contracts#build`
- `build` depends on `^build` (upstream dependencies)
- `test` depends on `^build`

---

## 6. Code Organization & Patterns

### 6.1 Module Organization
- **Explicit Exports:** No barrel indexes; direct file imports
- **Test Cohabitation:** `.test.ts` files live alongside source files
- **Provider-First Design:** Codex is the primary provider (only one currently supported)

### 6.2 Shared Utilities Pattern
- **models module:** Resolves and validates AI model slugs with aliases
- **git module:** Pure functions for sanitizing branch names
- **logging module:** Stateful rotating file sink
- **shell module:** Shell environment extraction
- **Net module:** Effect-based service for port availability checks

### 6.3 Type System
- **Effect Schema:** Runtime validation + type definitions
- **Branded Types:** Prevents mixing of semantically different ID types
- **Provider Abstraction:** Single `ProviderKind` type (currently "codex")

---

## 7. Key Dependencies & Versions

**Core:**
- `effect@8881a9b` (custom build from pkg.pr.new)
- `@effect/platform-node@8881a9b`
- `@effect/sql-sqlite-bun@8881a9b`
- `@effect/vitest@8881a9b`

**Web Stack:**
- `react@19.0.0`
- `@tanstack/react-router@1.160.2`
- `@tanstack/react-query@5.90.0`
- `tailwindcss@4.0.0`
- `zustand@5.0.11`

**Dev Tools:**
- `typescript@5.7.3`
- `vitest@4.0.0`
- `turbo@2.3.3`

---

## 8. Summary of Key Takeaways

1. **Monorepo Strategy:** Bun workspaces + Turbo - minimal, fast builds
2. **Type Safety:** Effect Schema for all contracts - runtime validation + types
3. **Styling:** Tailwind 4.0 + CSS variables for theming, no pre-built theme system
4. **Shared Utilities:** Small, focused modules with explicit exports (no barrels)
5. **Provider Abstraction:** Currently Codex-only, but contracts designed for future providers
6. **Testing:** Vitest cohabits tests with source files
7. **Build:** Source-first (no dist folder for shared/contracts except contracts which builds to CJS/ESM)

---

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a custom theme system to the T3 Code editor. Here's all the context I've gathered:

## Project Structure
- Monorepo with Bun workspaces + Turbo
- `apps/web` - React/Vite UI with Tailwind CSS 4.0, TanStack Router, Zustand
- `packages/shared` - shared runtime utilities with explicit subpath exports (no barrel index)
- `packages/contracts` - Effect/Schema type contracts

## Existing Theme System
The app already has a light/dark/system theme toggle:

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** (106 lines):
- Uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern (not context/provider)
- Stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in localStorage key `"t3code:theme"`
- Adds/removes `.dark` class on `<html>` element
- Handles system preference detection, cross-tab sync, transition suppression
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`

**`apps/web/src/index.css`** - CSS variables define the color system:
- `:root` defines light theme variables: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--card`, `--popover`, `--destructive`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--info`, `--success`, `--warning` (and their foreground variants)
- `@variant dark` overrides all variables for dark mode
- Uses `oklch()` color space for primary colors
- `@theme inline` block maps Tailwind `--color-*` to these variables
- All UI components reference these CSS variables through Tailwind classes

**Settings page (`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`):**
- Has an "Appearance" section with radio button cards for System/Light/Dark
- Uses `useTheme()` hook from `../hooks/useTheme`
- Shows resolved theme label

## `packages/shared` Pattern
Each module is a standalone `.ts` file in `src/`. The `package.json` exports each subpath explicitly:
```json
"exports": {
  "./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" },
  "./git": { "types": "./src/git.ts", "import": "./src/git.ts" },
  ...
}
```
Modules are pure functions and types. No Effect dependency required (some modules use it, some don't like `git.ts`).

## State Management Patterns
- `appSettings.ts`: Uses `useSyncExternalStore` + localStorage + Effect Schema validation
- `store.ts`: Zustand store with localStorage persistence
- `composerDraftStore.ts`: Zustand with `persist` middleware
- The `useTheme.ts` hook uses the simplest pattern: raw `useSyncExternalStore` with module-level state

## Requirements
1. Create `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` with:
   - Theme interface: `id`, `name`, `colors` (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar), `fontFamily`, `fontSize`
   - 3 built-in themes: "t3-dark" (default), "t3-light", "t3-midnight"
   - Export `getTheme(id)` and `listThemes()` functions
   
2. Theme provider/context in web app:
   - Store current theme in localStorage
   - `useTheme()` hook with `theme`, `setTheme`, `availableThemes`
   - Apply CSS custom properties from theme to document root

3. Theme selector component with preview swatches

4. Wire into existing component structure

## Key Design Decisions Needed:
1. **How to integrate with the existing light/dark/system toggle**: The existing system adds `.dark` class for Tailwind's dark variant. All existing CSS in `index.css` uses `@variant dark`. The new themes must work with this system. Options:
   - Each theme declares whether it's "light" or "dark" (a `colorScheme` field). When applied, we set/remove `.dark` accordingly.
   - Keep the existing System/Light/Dark toggle AND add theme selection (two separate settings).
   
2. **CSS variable mapping**: The theme defines 7 colors, but the app uses ~20+ CSS variables. The new theme colors should set the key ones, and we derive the rest (or the theme's `colors` should define all of them). The simplest approach: the theme sets its colors as custom properties AND also sets the existing CSS variables (--background, --foreground, etc.) to appropriate values so all existing components work.

3. **How to apply theme colors**: Override CSS variables on `document.documentElement.style` dynamically, which takes precedence over CSS stylesheet values.

## Constraints
- Do NOT add dependencies
- Do NOT modify build configuration
- Follow existing TypeScript/React patterns
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing CSS variable system
- `packages/shared` has no React dependency - it must stay framework-agnostic

Please design a detailed, file-by-file implementation plan.

## Requirements

2. Theme provider/context in web app:

## Key Design Decisions Needed:
1. **How to integrate with the existing light/dark/system toggle**: The existing system adds `.dark` class for Tailwind's dark variant. All existing CSS in `index.css` uses `@variant dark`. The new themes must work with this system. Options:

   - Keep the existing System/Light/Dark toggle AND add theme selection (two separate settings).
   
2. **CSS variable mapping**: The theme defines 7 colors, but the app uses ~20+ CSS variables. The new theme colors should set the key ones, and we derive the rest (or the theme's `colors` should define all of them). The simplest approach: the theme sets its colors as custom properties AND also sets the existing CSS variables (--background, --foreground, etc.) to appropriate values so all existing components work.

3. **How to apply theme colors**: Override CSS variables on `document.documentElement.style` dynamically, which takes precedence over CSS stylesheet values.

## Constraints
- Do NOT add dependencies
- Do NOT modify build configuration
- Follow existing TypeScript/React patterns
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing CSS variable system

4	 * All color values are self-contained CSS color literals (oklch, hex, rgb/rgba)
5	 * so they work both as inline style values and as swatch previews without

80	/**
81	 * Expand a `Theme` into a flat `Record<"--<name>", value>` suitable for
82	 * setting on `document.documentElement.style`.
83	 *
84	 * The seven `ThemeColors` slots are mapped to the full set of ~25 CSS custom
85	 * properties that the Tailwind layer expects. Derived values (card, muted,
86	 * border, etc.) are computed from the base colors using `color-mix()` so they
87	 * harmonize automatically.
88	 */
89	/** Derive a blended shade via CSS `color-mix()`. */
90	function mix(a: string, b: string, pct: number): string {
91	  return `color-mix(in srgb, ${a} ${pct}%, ${b})`;
92	}
93	
94	export function themeToCssProperties(theme: Theme): Record<string, string> {
95	  const { colors, colorScheme } = theme;
96	  const isDark = colorScheme === "dark";
97	
98	  const bg = colors.background;
99	  const fg = colors.foreground;
100	  const accent = colors.accent;
101	
102	  const card = isDark ? mix(bg, "#ffffff", 98) : bg;
103	  const popover = card;
104	  const muted = isDark ? `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` : `rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`;
105	  const mutedFg = isDark ? mix(fg, bg, 55) : mix(fg, bg, 55);
106	  const secondary = muted;
107	  const border = isDark ? `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` : `rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`;
108	  const input = isDark ? `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` : `rgba(0,0,0,0.10)`;
109	
110	  const props: Record<string, string> = {
111	    "--background": bg,
112	    "--foreground": fg,
113	    "--card": card,
114	    "--card-foreground": fg,
115	    "--popover": popover,
116	    "--popover-foreground": fg,
117	    "--primary": accent,
118	    "--primary-foreground": isDark ? "#ffffff" : "#ffffff",
119	    "--secondary": secondary,
120	    "--secondary-foreground": fg,
121	    "--muted": muted,
122	    "--muted-foreground": mutedFg,
123	    "--accent": muted,
124	    "--accent-foreground": fg,
125	    "--destructive": isDark
126	      ? "color-mix(in srgb, #ef4444 90%, #ffffff)"
127	      : "#ef4444",
128	    "--destructive-foreground": isDark ? "#f87171" : "#b91c1c",
129	    "--border": border,
130	    "--input": input,
131	    "--ring": accent,
132	    "--info": "#3b82f6",
133	    "--info-foreground": isDark ? "#60a5fa" : "#1d4ed8",
134	    "--success": "#10b981",
135	    "--success-foreground": isDark ? "#34d399" : "#047857",
136	    "--warning": "#f59e0b",
137	    "--warning-foreground": isDark ? "#fbbf24" : "#b45309",
138	    "--sidebar": colors.sidebar,
139	    "--editor": colors.editor,
140	    "--terminal": colors.terminal,
141	    "--status-bar": colors.statusBar,
142	  };
143	
144	  return props;
145	}

Everything is clean. Here's a summary of all the changes made:

---

## Summary of Changes

### New Files

1. **`packages/shared/src/themes.ts`** — Theme definition module
   - `ThemeColors` interface with 7 color slots: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `editor`, `terminal`, `statusBar`
   - `Theme` interface with `id`, `name`, `colorScheme`, `colors`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`
   - 3 built-in themes: **T3 Dark** (default), **T3 Light**, **T3 Midnight** (navy-tinted deep dark)
   - `getTheme(id)` — look up a theme by ID
   - `listThemes()` — return all built-in themes
   - `themeToCssProperties(theme)` — expand 7 theme colors into the full ~29 CSS custom properties the app consumes (derives card, popover, muted, border, etc.)
   - `getCssVarKeys()` — list all managed CSS variable names

2. **`packages/shared/src/themes.test.ts`** — 14 unit tests covering `listThemes`, `getTheme`, `themeToCssProperties`, and `getCssVarKeys`

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Theme selector component
   - Renders each available theme as a selectable radio card (matching existing settings UI pattern)
   - Shows 5-color swatch preview (background, sidebar, editor, accent, foreground)
   - Highlights the active theme with a "Selected" badge

### Modified Files

4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./themes"` subpath export

5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Replaced the light/dark/system toggle with full theme management
   - Stores theme ID in localStorage (`t3code:theme`)
   - Applies all CSS custom properties from the theme to `document.documentElement.style`
   - Toggles `.dark` class based on theme's `colorScheme` (Tailwind compat)
   - Flash prevention via synchronous module-load initialization
   - Cross-tab sync via `storage` event
   - Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme, availableThemes }`

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Updated Appearance section
   - Removed old `THEME_OPTIONS` array and inline radio-button loop
   - Replaced with `<ThemeSelector />` component
   - Shows active theme name instead of resolved "light"/"dark" label
